evidence.scientific_clarity: Table correctly shows 4 methods × 5 metrics with bold best/runner-up, group spans (Baselines/Ours), and subscript ±std formatting as required by brief.
evidence.typography: pdflatex produces Computer Modern (CMR/CMBX/CMTI/CMMI) Type 1 fonts, all embedded and subsetted; no DejaVu Sans; appropriate \small sizing.
evidence.palette: Black-and-white table with bold for directionality; no color palette needed or used; no misleading color choices.
evidence.layout_density: Table is rendered on a full letter-size page (612×792 pt), leaving substantial blank whitespace below the table; page is not cropped to table content.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption explains bold convention and seed count correctly, but caption.tex has a spurious extra closing brace '}}' that would cause a LaTeX compile error if included verbatim; no specific numerical finding cited.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is real pdfTeX vector output (v1.7), all fonts embedded as Type 1 subsets, fully selectable text confirmed via pdffonts.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, caption.tex, gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial; however table.tex (explicitly required by brief as standalone compilable LaTeX source) is absent from working directory.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption describes the bold convention and seed count but contains no specific numerical claim or highlighted finding (e.g., 'Method X achieves Y% on metric Z, outperforming baselines by N pp').

overall_strengths: Correct booktabs formatting with \toprule/\midrule/\bottomrule, no vertical lines, and proper group-spanning rows for Baselines vs Ours.
Real vector PDF with fully embedded Computer Modern fonts and accurate subscript-style mean±std formatting matching the brief's preferred camera-ready style.
overall_weaknesses: table.tex is missing from the working directory despite being an explicitly required output in the brief, and the full-letter page is not cropped to the table, leaving large blank whitespace in figure.pdf/figure.png.
Caption has a spurious extra '}}' brace that would break compilation, and lacks a concrete numerical finding to orient the reader.
